action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 7 segfaulted quickly (6.4s) with DataLoader on 1000 graphs. Test_env succeeded on 128 graphs, so crash is data-size related. Analysis: Possible cause is invalid edge indices (out-of-bounds) in some graphs, which would segfault in PyG's message passing. Small subset may avoid by chance. Decision: Run a diagnostic script to validate edge_index ranges for first 10000 graphs. If invalid graphs exist, filter them in training to prevent crashes. Then retrain on a small, clean subset to establish a baseline and measure iteration time.
